2026-04-21T05:31:39.0385569Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0386140Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0386558Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0386935Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0387238Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0387464Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0387624Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0387795Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0387974Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0388209Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0388409Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0388704Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:31:39.0388956Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. 2026-04-21T05:33:06.3084753Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T05:32:51.345471204Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58426","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-21T05:33:06.3112737Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T05:32:51.662355967Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58426,\"title\":\"[mgmt-generator] PATCH model with array of known mgmt type (e.g. WritableSubResource) at root emits invalid `Type.DeserializeType(...)` call\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nWhen a top-level resource update (PATCH body) model contains an array property of a known management type (e.g. `Azure.ResourceManager.Models.WritableSubResource`) **directly at the root** (no inner `properties` wrapper), the generated `Deserialize{Model}` method emits an invalid call to `WritableSubResource.DeserializeWritableSubResource(item, options)` — a method that is `internal` to `Azure.ResourceManager` and therefore not accessible from the generated assembly. This produces `CS0117: \\u0026#39;WritableSubResource\\u0026#39; does not contain a definition for \\u0026#39;DeserializeWritableSubResource\\u0026#39;`.\\n\\nThe same property type works correctly when:\\n- it lives on a regular (non-update) model, or\\n- it is nested inside an inner `*Properties` model (even if that `*Properties` is itself the body of a PATCH).\\n\\n## Repro (minimum TypeSpec)\\n\\n```tsp\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@typespec/rest\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@typespec/http\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Rest;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace\\n@service(#{ title: \\\\\\u0026#34;Repro\\\\\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Microsoft.Repro;\\n\\nenum Versions { @useDependency(Azure.ResourceManager.Versions.v1_0_Preview_1) v2024_01_01: \\\\\\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\\\\\u0026#34; }\\n\\nmodel FooResource is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\\\\\u0026#34;fooName\\\\\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\\\\\u0026#34;foos\\\\\\u0026#34;) @path\\n  name: string;\\n}\\n\\nmodel FooProperties {\\n  description?: string;\\n}\\n\\n// The trigger: array of WritableSubResource at the ROOT of the PATCH body\\n// (not nested inside an inner *Properties wrapper).\\nmodel FooPatch {\\n  endpoints?: Azure.ResourceManager.Models.WritableSubResource[];\\n  tags?: Record;\\n}\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Foos {\\n  get is ArmResourceRead;\\n  createOrUpdate is ArmResourceCreateOrReplaceAsync;\\n  update is ArmCustomPatchAsync;\\n  delete is ArmResourceDeleteWithoutOkAsync;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\n`FooPatch.Serialization.cs` deserializes each `endpoints` item via the same path used elsewhere for known mgmt types:\\n\\n```csharp\\narray.Add(ModelReaderWriter.Read(\\n    new BinaryData(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(item.GetRawText())),\\n    ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions,\\n    Generated.Context.Default));\\n```\\n\\n### Actual\\n\\n```csharp\\nList array = new List();\\nforeach (var item in prop.Value.EnumerateArray())\\n{\\n    array.Add(WritableSubResource.DeserializeWritableSubResource(item, options)); // CS0117\\n}\\n```\\n\\n(Note also: the per-item null check `if (item.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Null) array.Add(null)` is missing — symptom of the same root cause.)\\n\\n## Root cause analysis\\n\\nIn upstream `MrwSerializationTypeDefinition.CreateDeserializeValueExpression` (typespec/packages/http-client-csharp), only the `IsFrameworkType: true` branch delegates to `TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue`:\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar result = valueType switch\\n{\\n    { IsFrameworkType: true } when valueType.FrameworkType == typeof(Nullable\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;) =\\u0026gt;\\n        ScmCodeModelGenerator.Instance.TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue(...),\\n    { IsFrameworkType: true } =\\u0026gt;\\n        ScmCodeModelGenerator.Instance.TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue(...),\\n    { IsEnum: true } =\\u0026gt; ...,\\n    _ =\\u0026gt; GetDeserializationMethodInvocationForType(valueType, jsonElement, data, _mrwOptionsParameterSnippet) // ← falls here\\n};\\n```\\n\\nThe fallback `GetDeserializationMethodInvocationForType` emits `Static(type).Invoke(\\\\$\\\\\\u0026#34;Deserialize{type.Name}\\\\\\u0026#34;, ...)` directly and **never consults `TypeFactory`**. Therefore `ManagementTypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue`\\u0026#39;s name-based recovery (`KnownManagementTypes.IsKnownManagementType`) cannot rescue the call.\\n\\nFor the affected property only, the array\\u0026#39;s element `CSharpType` arrives at this method with `IsFrameworkType == false`, even though `KnownManagementTypes` maps the corresponding CLID (`Azure.ResourceManager.Models.WritableSubResource`) to `typeof(WritableSubResource)`. The other 3 sites in dnsresolver that use the same `InputModelType` (id 368, same CLID) get the framework type correctly. Symmetric evidence on the serialize side — the per-item null-check is also omitted only in the broken case (`TypeRequiresNullCheckInSerialization` requires `IsFrameworkType == true` for non-value reference types).\\n\\nThe exact transformation that strips the framework flag for this one property is not yet pinned down (ruled out: `FlattenPropertyVisitor`, `InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor`, `NameVisitor`, TCGC `@@alternateType` not being applied — there\\u0026#39;s only one InputModelType for `WritableSubResource` with the canonical CLID).\\n\\n## Suggested fix directions\\n\\n1. **Quick, mgmt-side**: add a visitor in the mgmt generator that walks update-model property types (and their element types) and forces framework `CSharpType` for any CLID in `KnownManagementTypes._idToSystemTypeMap`, so `IsFrameworkType` is true by the time serialization is built.\\n2. **Upstream**: have `GetDeserializationMethodInvocationForType` consult `TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue` (or have `CreateDeserializeValueExpression` route the `_` branch through `TypeFactory`), so the name-based recovery in mgmt\\u0026#39;s override is reachable.\\n\\n## Affected package\\n\\n`sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver` — currently worked around in `src/Custom/DnsForwardingRulesetPatch.cs` via a `[CodeGenSerialization(...DeserializationValueHook=...)]` hook.\\n\\n## Repro command\\n\\nFrom a clone of the repro: `cd ; npx tsp-client update; dotnet build` → CS0117.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58426\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-21T05:30:13Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-21T05:31:51Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-21T05:33:06.3136935Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T05:32:51.680477815Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58426,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-21T05:33:06.3164481Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T05:32:51.936538583Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58426,\"title\":\"[mgmt-generator] PATCH model with array of known mgmt type (e.g. WritableSubResource) at root emits invalid `Type.DeserializeType(...)` call\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nWhen a top-level resource update (PATCH body) model contains an array property of a known management type (e.g. `Azure.ResourceManager.Models.WritableSubResource`) **directly at the root** (no inner `properties` wrapper), the generated `Deserialize{Model}` method emits an invalid call to `WritableSubResource.DeserializeWritableSubResource(item, options)` — a method that is `internal` to `Azure.ResourceManager` and therefore not accessible from the generated assembly. This produces `CS0117: \\u0026#39;WritableSubResource\\u0026#39; does not contain a definition for \\u0026#39;DeserializeWritableSubResource\\u0026#39;`.\\n\\nThe same property type works correctly when:\\n- it lives on a regular (non-update) model, or\\n- it is nested inside an inner `*Properties` model (even if that `*Properties` is itself the body of a PATCH).\\n\\n## Repro (minimum TypeSpec)\\n\\n```tsp\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@typespec/rest\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@typespec/http\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\\\\\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\\\\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Rest;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace\\n@service(#{ title: \\\\\\u0026#34;Repro\\\\\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Microsoft.Repro;\\n\\nenum Versions { @useDependency(Azure.ResourceManager.Versions.v1_0_Preview_1) v2024_01_01: \\\\\\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\\\\\u0026#34; }\\n\\nmodel FooResource is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\\\\\u0026#34;fooName\\\\\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\\\\\u0026#34;foos\\\\\\u0026#34;) @path\\n  name: string;\\n}\\n\\nmodel FooProperties {\\n  description?: string;\\n}\\n\\n// The trigger: array of WritableSubResource at the ROOT of the PATCH body\\n// (not nested inside an inner *Properties wrapper).\\nmodel FooPatch {\\n  endpoints?: Azure.ResourceManager.Models.WritableSubResource[];\\n  tags?: Record;\\n}\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface Foos {\\n  get is ArmResourceRead;\\n  createOrUpdate is ArmResourceCreateOrReplaceAsync;\\n  update is ArmCustomPatchAsync;\\n  delete is ArmResourceDeleteWithoutOkAsync;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\n`FooPatch.Serialization.cs` deserializes each `endpoints` item via the same path used elsewhere for known mgmt types:\\n\\n```csharp\\narray.Add(ModelReaderWriter.Read(\\n    new BinaryData(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(item.GetRawText())),\\n    ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions,\\n    Generated.Context.Default));\\n```\\n\\n### Actual\\n\\n```csharp\\nList array = new List();\\nforeach (var item in prop.Value.EnumerateArray())\\n{\\n    array.Add(WritableSubResource.DeserializeWritableSubResource(item, options)); // CS0117\\n}\\n```\\n\\n(Note also: the per-item null check `if (item.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Null) array.Add(null)` is missing — symptom of the same root cause.)\\n\\n## Root cause analysis\\n\\nIn upstream `MrwSerializationTypeDefinition.CreateDeserializeValueExpression` (typespec/packages/http-client-csharp), only the `IsFrameworkType: true` branch delegates to `TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue`:\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar result = valueType switch\\n{\\n    { IsFrameworkType: true } when valueType.FrameworkType == typeof(Nullable\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;) =\\u0026gt;\\n        ScmCodeModelGenerator.Instance.TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue(...),\\n    { IsFrameworkType: true } =\\u0026gt;\\n        ScmCodeModelGenerator.Instance.TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue(...),\\n    { IsEnum: true } =\\u0026gt; ...,\\n    _ =\\u0026gt; GetDeserializationMethodInvocationForType(valueType, jsonElement, data, _mrwOptionsParameterSnippet) // ← falls here\\n};\\n```\\n\\nThe fallback `GetDeserializationMethodInvocationForType` emits `Static(type).Invoke(\\\\$\\\\\\u0026#34;Deserialize{type.Name}\\\\\\u0026#34;, ...)` directly and **never consults `TypeFactory`**. Therefore `ManagementTypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue`\\u0026#39;s name-based recovery (`KnownManagementTypes.IsKnownManagementType`) cannot rescue the call.\\n\\nFor the affected property only, the array\\u0026#39;s element `CSharpType` arrives at this method with `IsFrameworkType == false`, even though `KnownManagementTypes` maps the corresponding CLID (`Azure.ResourceManager.Models.WritableSubResource`) to `typeof(WritableSubResource)`. The other 3 sites in dnsresolver that use the same `InputModelType` (id 368, same CLID) get the framework type correctly. Symmetric evidence on the serialize side — the per-item null-check is also omitted only in the broken case (`TypeRequiresNullCheckInSerialization` requires `IsFrameworkType == true` for non-value reference types).\\n\\nThe exact transformation that strips the framework flag for this one property is not yet pinned down (ruled out: `FlattenPropertyVisitor`, `InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor`, `NameVisitor`, TCGC `@@alternateType` not being applied — there\\u0026#39;s only one InputModelType for `WritableSubResource` with the canonical CLID).\\n\\n## Suggested fix directions\\n\\n1. **Quick, mgmt-side**: add a visitor in the mgmt generator that walks update-model property types (and their element types) and forces framework `CSharpType` for any CLID in `KnownManagementTypes._idToSystemTypeMap`, so `IsFrameworkType` is true by the time serialization is built.\\n2. **Upstream**: have `GetDeserializationMethodInvocationForType` consult `TypeFactory.DeserializeJsonValue` (or have `CreateDeserializeValueExpression` route the `_` branch through `TypeFactory`), so the name-based recovery in mgmt\\u0026#39;s override is reachable.\\n\\n## Affected package\\n\\n`sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver` — currently worked around in `src/Custom/DnsForwardingRulesetPatch.cs` via a `[CodeGenSerialization(...DeserializationValueHook=...)]` hook.\\n\\n## Repro command\\n\\nFrom a clone of the repro: `cd ; npx tsp-client update; dotnet build` → CS0117.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58426\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-21T05:30:13Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-21T05:31:51Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
